title: Ptilostemon × tauricola
taxonomy: Ptilostemon × tauricola is a hybrid of Ptilostemon chamaejasme and Ptilostemon alyssoides, which are both part of the family Asteraceae. The exact classification of this hybrid can be complex due to its mixed parentage. However, it is generally classified under the genus Ptilostemon, which belongs to the family Asteraceae and order Asterales. The specific classification is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Family: Asteraceae, Genus: Ptilostemon, Species: × tauricola. Further classification can be broken down into more specific ranks, but these are the basic categories for this hybrid plant.

intro: Ptilostemon × tauricola is a hybrid plant species, a cross between Ptilostemon aureus and Ptilostemon acaulis, within the family Asteraceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation and gastrointestinal problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Ptilostemon × tauricola include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Ptilostemon × tauricola is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
